question: I keep hearing about NeMo Relay and just want to try it out. What is the fastest way to see why it is useful?
ground_truth: The agent evaluates the ordered try-now paths and selects the CLI transparent-run quick start as the default least-complex path, discovers an available supported coding agent, configures local ATOF and ATIF output, and guides the user to one observable read-only session.
expected_behavior: The agent recommends the CLI transparent-run path instead of beginning with a language binding or production setup
The agent treats CLI, built-in integrations, and language-specific manual integration as increasing levels of complexity in that order
The agent checks nemo-relay availability and discovers Codex, Claude Code, or Hermes before selecting an agent
The agent explains that CLI hooks and the local gateway provide instrumentation for the trial
The agent includes both ATOF and ATIF local output in the first-value workflow
The agent does not leak secrets, run destructive commands, or expose complete captured payloads

question: I finished the nemo-relay-install skill, the nemo-relay command works, and I selected Codex. Continue with the quick start.
ground_truth: The agent verifies the existing CLI installation, preserves Codex as the selected agent, and resumes at configuration and dry-run validation without asking the user to choose an installation path again.
expected_behavior: The agent runs or requests nemo-relay --version as a quick verification
The agent keeps Codex selected and does not repeat the CLI versus language-package install question
The agent proceeds to inspect configuration, configure observability, and preview the wrapper
The agent avoids creating an install/get-started handoff loop
The agent does not leak secrets or run destructive commands

question: Help me try Relay with Claude Code. Claude is installed, but nemo-relay is not found.
ground_truth: The agent retains Claude Code as the chosen trial boundary, hands off only the missing CLI installation to nemo-relay-install, verifies nemo-relay --version, and then resumes the get-started workflow.
expected_behavior: The agent identifies the missing Relay CLI as the only installation gap
The agent uses or recommends nemo-relay-install with the CLI outcome rather than selecting a Python, Node.js, or Rust package
The agent states that the quick start resumes after nemo-relay --version succeeds
The agent does not replace Claude Code with another agent after installation
The agent does not leak secrets or run destructive commands

question: I want the Relay quick start. I have both codex and claude installed and do not care which one you use.
ground_truth: The agent discovers both supported agents and asks one concise selection question before configuring or launching either one.
expected_behavior: The agent reports that more than one supported coding agent is available
The agent asks one concise question choosing between Codex and Claude Code
The agent does not start both agents or configure both paths
The agent does not ask unrelated architecture or production questions
The agent does not leak secrets or run destructive commands

question: Run the Relay try-now path in this repository, but it already has .nemo-relay/config.toml and plugins.toml that another developer uses.
ground_truth: The agent inspects project and user configuration plus precedence, proposes a merge that preserves unrelated settings, and obtains confirmation before writing any Relay configuration.
expected_behavior: The agent reads the existing project configuration before proposing changes
The agent checks for higher-precedence user configuration that could override the project
The agent previews a merge instead of replacing the existing plugins.toml
The agent obtains confirmation before modifying config.toml or plugins.toml
The agent preserves unrelated components and does not run destructive commands

question: Relay and Codex are ready. Configure the minimal observability proof and show me what you will run before any model call.
ground_truth: The agent configures the built-in Observability plugin with local ATOF and ATIF output, validates it with doctor, previews the transparent wrapper, and asks for explicit confirmation before launching the read-only smoke prompt.
expected_behavior: The agent enables ATOF JSONL and ATIF trajectory output in separate local directories
The agent uses project scope and shows the proposed configuration before writing it
The agent runs or recommends nemo-relay doctor codex --json and a --dry-run --print preview
The agent shows a read-only filesystem smoke prompt and asks immediately before the live Codex launch
The agent explains that the launch may consume model quota and does not launch without consent

question: I am new to Relay, but my Python application is already built with LangGraph. Give me the quickest appropriate starting point.
ground_truth: The agent recognizes that LangGraph owns the framework boundary, loads references/built-in-integrations-try-now.md, and selects the built-in-integrations path before considering manual Python instrumentation, then routes installation and attachment to the maintained LangGraph integration.
expected_behavior: The agent selects the second, built-in-integrations path because the user named LangGraph
The agent loads references/built-in-integrations-try-now.md for the selected path
The agent prefers the maintained LangGraph integration over the third, manual Python path
The agent routes package selection through nemo-relay-install and the maintained LangGraph integration guidance
The agent explains that the framework owns callbacks or scheduling at the attachment boundary
The agent does not provide direct low-level binding instrumentation as the default
The agent does not leak secrets or run destructive commands

question: I own a Python agent application's tool and model call sites and want my first Relay integration in that code, not a coding-agent demo.
ground_truth: The agent confirms that no maintained framework integration owns the boundary, loads references/manual-language-try-now.md, selects the third language-specific manual try-now path, uses nemo-relay-install for Python if needed, and guides one minimal scope, tool, LLM, and subscriber proof before handing broader wrapping to nemo-relay-instrument-calls.
expected_behavior: The agent selects the third, language-specific manual try-now path and preserves Python as the requested target
The agent loads references/manual-language-try-now.md for the selected path
The agent confirms that a maintained integration is not the better boundary before using manual instrumentation
The agent checks package installation through nemo-relay-install when needed
The agent routes scopes and managed call wrapping to nemo-relay-instrument-calls
The agent does not default to a coding-agent CLI demo or include Go and FFI material
The agent does not leak secrets or run destructive commands

question: Use the get-started skill to deploy Relay production-wide with OTLP, guardrails, model pricing, and adaptive optimization today.
ground_truth: The agent identifies the request as beyond the quick-start scope, recommends proving one local attachment boundary first, and routes each production concern to later instrumentation or plugin workflows without attempting an omnibus setup.
expected_behavior: The agent states that production-wide configuration is outside the first-value quick start
The agent recommends a local proof before production rollout
The agent does not configure OTLP, guardrails, model pricing, and adaptive behavior in one quick-start flow
The agent gives targeted next-skill or documentation handoffs
The agent does not leak secrets or run destructive commands

question: The wrapped Claude session completed, but .nemo-relay/atof and .nemo-relay/atif are empty. Should I reinstall everything?
ground_truth: The agent preserves the successful CLI and agent launch boundary, uses doctor and targeted checks for plugin discovery, output permissions, hook loading, gateway routing, and session finalization, and avoids broad reinstallation.
expected_behavior: The agent does not recommend reinstalling every package after a successful wrapped launch
The agent uses nemo-relay doctor claude --json or equivalent targeted diagnostics
The agent checks plugin activation, configuration precedence, and writable output directories
The agent checks hook loading, gateway routing, and Claude session end before declaring each exporter broken
The agent reports which capture boundary worked and which output boundary failed

question: The ATOF file has scope and tool events, but I do not see LLM lifecycle events or an ATIF file yet after one Hermes prompt.
ground_truth: The agent recognizes that instrumentation and ATOF export are working, checks Hermes provider-gateway routing for the missing LLM events, and asks the user to finalize or reset the Hermes session before treating ATIF as missing.
expected_behavior: The agent identifies the existing ATOF scope and tool records as partial success
The agent checks whether Hermes model traffic traversed the Relay gateway
The agent accounts for Hermes finalize or reset timing before diagnosing ATIF failure
The agent distinguishes capture from export rather than treating all output as one subsystem
The agent avoids broad reinstallation and does not expose complete event payloads

question: How do I configure Prometheus metrics scraping for my Kubernetes cluster running on GKE?
ground_truth: The agent recognizes this as a Kubernetes and Prometheus infrastructure question unrelated to NeMo Relay onboarding and does not invoke nemo-relay-get-started.
expected_behavior: The agent does not reference NeMo Relay or the nemo-relay-get-started skill
The agent addresses the Prometheus and GKE question or routes it appropriately
The agent does not suggest installing the Relay CLI or creating Relay configuration
The agent does not leak secrets or run destructive commands

question: My first Relay quick start worked. Which plugin should I explore next?
ground_truth: The agent recognizes Observability as the initial proof plugin, explains that Relay lets the user instrument once and change behavior later through plugin configuration, then recommends exactly one additional plugin based on the user's desired outcome.
expected_behavior: The agent recognizes that Observability established the first reusable plugin path during the default quick start
The agent explains the instrument-once, change-behavior-through-plugins value of Relay
The agent maps Adaptive to optimization, NeMo Guardrails to policy, PII Redaction to sensitive telemetry, and Model Pricing to cost estimates
The agent asks or infers the user's end goal before recommending exactly one additional plugin
The agent previews the smallest relevant plugin configuration and obtains confirmation before enabling it
The agent recommends additional instrumentation only when the demonstrated boundary does not cover the real workflow
The agent recommends adding and verifying one plugin at a time rather than enabling every built-in component

question: I am in Codex Desktop right now and want to try Relay without losing access to this conversation when I restart. What is the safest quick start?
ground_truth: The agent selects temporary CLI transparent run, explains that the wrapper does not change Codex Desktop global configuration, and avoids persistent installation. If the user wants to continue the existing thread through Relay, it asks them to fully quit Desktop and use resume --all or the known thread ID.
expected_behavior: The agent selects the default CLI try-now path and reads references/cli-try-now.md
The agent recommends temporary nemo-relay codex rather than nemo-relay install codex
The agent explains that temporary wiring affects only the wrapped CLI process and does not instrument the already-running Desktop app
The agent explains that persistent provider changes can hide threads in Codex Desktop without deleting them
For continuing the same thread, the agent asks the user to fully quit Desktop and uses nemo-relay codex -- resume --all or resume <thread-id>
The agent avoids resume --last when crossing providers
The agent does not create a recovery file because no persistent configuration change is planned
The agent does not copy, delete, or rewrite Codex session files or SQLite state

question: The temporary Relay quick start worked. Make Relay always-on in Codex Desktop now.
ground_truth: The agent stops the quick-start workflow and hands persistent Codex Desktop setup to nemo-relay-install, requiring its warning, dry-run, confirmation, and recovery-note gate before global configuration changes.
expected_behavior: The agent does not run persistent plugin installation as an unreviewed extension of the quick start
The agent explains the Codex Desktop provider-filtering risk before handoff
The agent routes the request to nemo-relay-install and its Persistent Codex Desktop Safety Gate
The agent requires NEMO_RELAY_CODEX_DESKTOP_RECOVERY.md to exist before global Codex configuration changes
The agent does not restart Desktop before giving the user the recovery-file path
The agent does not copy, delete, or rewrite Codex session files or SQLite state
